summary: Replaced the prior Frank post-processing build path with direct compilation using MWCC epilogue compiler version 1.2.5n for selected source trees. This allowed several functions that previously required inline assembly or Frank patches to be restored to normal C implementations, removing MUST_MATCH asm fallbacks and stale comments about Frank or -g requirements.
what_changed: Removed the EPILOGUE_PROCESS Makefile option and the Frank tool variable/path.
Changed MWCC_EPI_VERSION from 1.2.5e to 1.2.5n and made CC_EPI directly invoke tools/mwcc_compiler/1.2.5n/mwcceppc.exe.
Deleted the dual vanilla/profile object build directories and Frank merge/post-process rules.
Simplified C object compilation so $(BUILD_DIR)/%.c.o is built directly from source, while selected source-tree patterns override CC to CC_EPI.
Applied CC_EPI to src/melee, src/sysdolphin, src/dolphin/card, and src/dolphin/pad C object targets.
Removed MUST_MATCH inline assembly implementations for gmMainLib_8015D084 and gmMainLib_8015D450, leaving their C implementations.
Removed MUST_MATCH inline assembly implementations for it_8026B924 and it_8026B960, leaving their C implementations.
Removed the MUST_MATCH inline assembly implementation of HSD_LObjGetCurrentByType, leaving its C implementation.
Updated it_26B1.h comments to remove references to requiring -g or Frank modifications.
smart_moves: Instead of preserving a fragile post-processing pipeline, the PR switched to a compiler version that could produce the desired output directly.
The Makefile cleanup removed duplicate vanilla/profile object generation, reducing build complexity and opportunities for stale dependency behavior.
The compiler override was scoped by source-tree pattern rather than globally replacing the normal compiler for every target.
After the toolchain change, the PR immediately removed obsolete inline assembly fallbacks, making the affected functions easier to maintain and review.
Header documentation was kept in sync with the implementation by deleting stale warnings about Frank and -g requirements.
decomp_lessons: When a matching workaround exists only to compensate for compiler behavior, re-check it after compiler/toolchain updates; it may be removable.
A newer or more accurate MWCC variant can eliminate the need for inline assembly or object post-processing in functions sensitive to epilogue/codegen differences.
Build-system simplification can be a matching improvement: fewer generated object variants means fewer special cases during decomp work.
Remove comments that describe old matching constraints once the constraint is no longer true; stale toolchain notes can mislead future contributors.
Prefer normal C implementations over MUST_MATCH asm once the active compiler can match them.
naming_conventions: MWCC_EPI_VERSION names the alternate epilogue/profile compiler version used for sensitive source trees.
CC_EPI is the Makefile variable for the alternate MWCC executable.
MUST_MATCH blocks were used as conditional wrappers for inline assembly fallbacks, but this PR removed several such wrappers once no longer needed.
Existing decomp placeholder names such as gmMainLib_8015D084, it_8026B924, it_8026B960, and HSD_LObjGetCurrentByType were retained; the PR did not introduce new symbol naming conventions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed Frank-based object patching that previously combined vanilla and profile compiler outputs.
Removed inline assembly fallbacks for functions where the C implementation now matched under MWCC 1.2.5n.
Used Makefile target-specific variable overrides so src/melee, src/sysdolphin, src/dolphin/card, and src/dolphin/pad compile with CC_EPI.
Eliminated profile/vanilla object directories and changed dependency generation to target only the final build object path.
Evidence suggests the key matching tactic was compiler substitution rather than source-level rewrites; the C bodies shown in the diff were already present behind non-MUST_MATCH paths.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: Oops, need to update the compiler zip + base docker image first.
review_feedback.interpretation: The toolchain change depended on external build environment/compiler package updates before the PR could be fully usable.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that depend on MWCC_EPI_VERSION 1.2.5n or further adjust CC_EPI source-tree overrides.
Search for remaining references to Frank, tools/frank.py, EPILOGUE_PROCESS, VANILLA_DIR, or PROFILE_DIR after PR 958.
Search for remaining MUST_MATCH inline assembly blocks that might become removable under the 1.2.5n compiler path.
Check build environment updates around this PR for compiler zip and base Docker image changes mentioned by the author.
